sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 11

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 11:1
items.html:
(to the question) Bi'ah Kadma'a [first coming]
Every time MAN [Mayin Nukvin] rise from some degree, they must first rise to the one above their upper one — that is, to the third degree above them — and there the first Zivug [coupling] is made upon them. From there they descend with their upper one to its place below, and a second Zivug is made upon them in their own upper one, called the Zivug of Ibur [conception]. This conduct applies to every kind of ascent of MAN in the world: whether at the time of their exit from the Klipot [shells], when the upper one raises them, or at the time when they already have Nefesh-Ruach, when the lower one rises to the upper one by itself — the above rule always applies: first they must rise to the one above the upper one to receive the first Zivug there, and afterwards they come into their own upper one for the second Zivug, to be conceived. For this reason the first Zivug is called Bi'ah Kadma'a, meaning the first coming, in which they do not yet receive the form of an embryo [Ubar]; and the second Zivug is called the second coming [Bi'ah Shniya], in which they receive the form of the embryo. This is the secret of the saying that a woman does not conceive from the first coming. (p. 738, Item 7; and p. 744, Item 13).
